rationale.R1.1: The artifact advances explicit thesis and contribution claims (deployment-gap framework, new failure mode, generalisation beyond V2G); evaluating whether these are coherently framed falls centrally in scope for any reasonable reading of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.2: The argument is structured as three numbered observations leading to a causal mechanism; checking whether each step follows from the prior and whether the conclusion is warranted is a natural duty when reviewing this kind of analytical text.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims (≈90 % commitment, two-of-three markets, 4-hour window) and mechanistic claims (state resets, fixed multiplier) are asserted but no figures or tables are visible; alignment between these claims and their evidential basis must be flagged as unverifiable or at risk.
rationale.R1.4: The text makes prominent novelty claims ('contributes to the growing literature', 'formalizes a new failure mode', 'extends the safe RL paradigm'); assessing whether the delta over prior work is accurately characterised is a central duty.
rationale.R1.6: Writing review is the most common reading of the bare directive 'check this' applied to prose; sentence-level and paragraph-level quality is therefore centrally in scope under this interpretation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on the technical and logical soundness of the argument (evidence–claim alignment, novelty, mechanism validity), or primarily on prose and writing quality?
